title: Sloanea ledermannii
taxonomy: Sloanea ledermannii belongs to the family Ebenaceae. The family Ebenaceae is a small family of flowering plants within the order Oxalidales. Sloanea ledermannii is native to New Guinea and is a tree species. It is classified as a species of evergreen tree. The species Sloanea ledermannii is a part of the genus Sloanea.
uses: Sloanea ledermannii is a plant species with reported medicinal uses. Its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. In some regions, Sloanea ledermannii is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
